title: azure-rate-limit-deepseek-persistence
description:
This is a continuation of azure-rate-limit-deepseek-spread. In this window, two additional persona runs failed with the same explicit provider-side error from `azure/deepseek-v4-pro`: `RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit.` The failures affected different story/persona pairs (`story 24 / dev` and `story 15 / test_implementer`) and each consumed several minutes of wall time before failing, indicating the previously escalated Azure quota condition remains active rather than having self-resolved.

The immediate impact is reduced throughput on active stories and repeated long-running failed work on the deepseek path. In this minute there is no evidence of a per-story retry storm, queue skew, or tick stall, so the failure mode is broad but not yet shown to have halted the whole factory. The error text is diagnostically clear and attributable to provider or model-capacity conditions rather than story-domain logic.

A secondary signal suggests continuing state drift around worktrees: a new `worktree_create` event appeared for `story_id` 61 while the watcher note reports this as an additional missing-ID worktree, increasing the count from five to six. That drift is not strongly tied to the Azure failures from the raw evidence in this window, but it does indicate concurrent cleanup or state-tracking inconsistency.

Overall, the clearest concern remains the sustained Azure rate-limit/quota failure pattern across multiple watcher intervals and multiple personas. The worktree issue is worth noting for continuity, but the stronger, operator-relevant pattern in this bundle is the unresolved provider-side capacity failure on deepseek runs.

evidence.summary_excerpt: The Azure deepseek-v4-pro quota/rate-limit failure escalated in the prior two notes continued again this window, with two more long persona runs failing on stories 24 and 15 with the same diagnostically clear token-limit error.

evidence.excerpt: persona=dev model=azure/deepseek-v4-pro duration_s=284.201 success=false error="...RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0."
persona=test_implementer model=azure/deepseek-v4-pro duration_s=233.179 success=false error="...RateLimitError - Rate limit exceeded. Quota request exceeds the tokens limit. Requested tokens: 0."

{"noteworthy":"Two failed runs in-window repeated the same clear infrastructure-side Azure RateLimitError/token quota condition already escalated in prior notes, now affecting story 24 dev and story 15 test_implementer."}
{"noteworthy":"Failures are distributed across two story/persona pairs with count 1 each, so no retry storm formed despite the repeated underlying error across windows."}

{"noteworthy":"Missing-ID worktrees increased from five in the prior note to six, with a new missing story 61 worktree appearing in the raw git stream; this looks like continuing cleanup/state drift rather than a transient one-off."}

escalation_reason: Azure deepseek-v4-pro quota/rate-limit failures have now persisted across multiple watcher intervals, including this window from 2026-05-30T03:07:33+00:00 to 2026-05-30T03:08:27+00:00 with two more multi-minute failures on different stories/personas. The error class is explicit and factory-attributable to provider/model capacity selection rather than story logic, so L3 judgment is needed to assess the ongoing deepseek routing/capacity issue and the parallel worktree-state drift.
